chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS Crl. Bail Application No.S-53 of 2026 Applicants: 1. Ali Ahmed @ Ahmed Ali s/o Ali Murad Rajar. 2. Asad Ali s/o Sain Bux alias Sanwal Rajar Through Mr. Jeeloji Rajput, Advocate. Respondent: The State. Through Mr. Ghulam Abbas Dalwani, D.P.G. Complainant: Abdul Quddus s/o Daim Khan Through Mr. Afzal Kareem Virk, Advocate. Date of hearing: 18.02.2026 Date of Order: 18.02.2026 O R D E R Miran Muhammad Shah, J: Through this Order, I intend to dispose of above-mentioned bail application, whereby the applicants/ accused seek post arrest bail in F.I.R No.279 of 2025 for offence punishable under sections 395, 397 PPC (sections declared in final medical certificates of injured as 337-A(i), 337-F(i), 337-F(vi), 337-L PPC) of P.S Khipro, after rejection of their bail plea by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Khipro, vide Order dated 10-01-2026. 2. The details and particulars of the F.I.R are already available in bail application and the F.I.R, as such; need not to reproduce the same hereunder. 3. Heard and perused. 4. The affidavit has been filed by the complainant of the case, wherein he has stated that he has no objection if this Court grants bail to both the applicants as compromise talks are continuing and in order to reach at final settlement, release of the present applicants/accused on bail is required; such affidavit is taken on 2 record and based upon the said affidavit, which is affirmed by the complainant, who is present in Court, the present applicants/ accused are granted bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/= (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) each and P.R.Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of learned trial Court 5.

Heard and perused. 4. The affidavit has been filed by the complainant of the case, wherein he has stated that he has no objection if this Court grants bail to both the applicants as compromise talks are continuing and in order to reach at final settlement, release of the present applicants/accused on bail is required; such affidavit is taken on 2 record and based upon the said affidavit, which is affirmed by the complainant, who is present in Court, the present applicants/ accused are granted bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/= (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) each and P.R.Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of learned trial Court 5. The instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. JUDGE *Saleem*
